item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, tyrannosaurs actually didn't have feathers - there's actually skin imprints that showed T-rex was scaly, much like elephants don't have fur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its not that its harder to believe, it's just that the reasoning behind it kinda makes me mad. it's almost circlejerking at this point. and yeah i don't like 4041 for the same reasons. making a story about dinosaurs building a spaceship is technically feasible, we don't have evidence that they didn't, but we do have evidence that they had feathers. that's the difference. i just think its a bit disrespectful to the people who work hard putting together these animals and trying to get rid of the idea that dinosaurs were big lizards when stories about them not having feathers and making the people who made those discoveries 'wrong'.

4041 comes across as almost… cynical. almost like a tongue in cheek story about how the author doesn't like the idea of dinosaurs with feathers. its something that happens way too often, and that's why i don't like it. 'dinosaur fans' treat paleontology like a franchise, like making a discovery could be a wrong decision or something that the 'fanbase' doesn't like, but science doesn't work that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you read the linked SCP (SCP-4041)? It will probably piss you off as well, but there's the narrative reason for the featherlessness, at least.

That aside, this is a fictional universe where sometimes the sun has a sister, humans overthrew Bigfoot, breakfast isn't real, and the Byzantine Empire was secretly aliens. I'm not sure why it's easier to accept that dinosaurs were able to build a spaceship, than that they looked a little different than our evidence in the real world suggests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
im pretty torn on this, for one i really enjoy the references to 317, but the footnote makes no sense out of and in universe.

rant warning!

can people please just grow up and accept that most, if not all theropods had feathers? dinosaurs aren't movie monsters, they were living animals. they were birds. end of story. there is a numerous amount of evidence of theropods possessing feathers. even all dinosaurs still alive today have feathers. i understand that trying to piece together a living creature out of just its bones is difficult but please don't use that difficulty as an excuse to make them whatever you want, especially when it should be common knowledge. i know this has little to do with the article but i needed to get this off my chest.

relevant xkcd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dinosaurs getting revenge on those bastard asteroids that tried to take them out? I love it! Btw, I get what you were going for with the mural of the moon thing with the face but I just read SCP-4220 and can’t help but think of that, lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy the ever-increasing number of advanced prehistoric animal civilizations on this website.

Now, the real question is, could the advanced dinosaur civilization beat the advanced bigfoot civilization in a fight?

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
I am quite honored
To receive this lovely gift
A Gabe Jade haiku

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"1. No instances possess feathers"

Well of course not - the cryogenic process would have made them all fall out.
Like Doctor Evil's Mr. Bigglesworth losing all his fur in Austin Powers. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dinosaurs… in… SPAAAAAAAACE!

In all seriousness, this was a good mixture of sci-fi and weirdness. Adding to the fact that the brevity of the piece doesn't cause it to overstay its welcome, and this is a fun article.

+1. All dinos man your battle stations.

Come on, sail the stars.
See vengeful space dinosaurs
hunting down their prey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Technically a setup piece, but it better be neat enough to stand on its own indefinitely because the last drop of writing juice left my body when I clicked Save.

Thanks to stormbreath, Geckoguy2704 does not match any existing user name, Captain Kirby for reading, as well as MaliceAforethought for a great suggestion.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Galileo_Gaspra_Mosaic.jpg public domain
